title: Rob Gurzeev on Why AI Has Changed Cybersecurity’s Biggest Blind Spot
excerpt: Artificial intelligence is transforming how software is built, deployed, and secured. While AI has accelerated innovation across industries, it has also expanded the number of internet-facing assets organizations need to protect. According to Rob Gurzeev, CEO and Co-Founder of CyCognito, the challenge is no longer just identifying known vulnerabilities. It is understanding what is actually […]
content_html:
TL;DR

AI makes it trivially easy for anyone in an organization to deploy internet-facing applications, often outside established security processes. CyCognito CEO Rob Gurzeev says the resulting blind spots are more dangerous than known vulnerabilities. His answer: continuous, outside-in attack-surface mapping that validates what is actually exploitable rather than scanning a known asset list.

Artificial intelligence is transforming how software is built, deployed, and secured. While AI has accelerated innovation across industries, it has also expanded the number of internet-facing assets organizations need to protect. According to Rob Gurzeev, CEO and Co-Founder of CyCognito, the challenge is no longer just identifying known vulnerabilities. It is understanding what is actually exposed, how those assets connect, and how attackers can exploit them.

Drawing on years of experience in cybersecurity and intelligence, Gurzeev believes many organizations still operate with an incomplete view of their attack surface. That gap, he says, is becoming more dangerous as AI makes it easier than ever to create and expose new applications.

A security mindset built on finding the unknown

Gurzeev’s path into cybersecurity began long before AI entered the picture. As a teenager, he spent time exploring computers and Internet Relay Chat (IRC) communities, where curiosity about hacking first took hold. That interest eventually led him to an intelligence unit, where he worked on reconnaissance and attack-surface operations.

“Honestly, this work chose me more than I chose it,” Gurzeev said. He explained that the role often started with little more than a name and required finding “the path of least resistance into something that mattered.”

Those experiences continue to shape how he approaches cybersecurity today. “I was taught you never actually know what reality is. You have to go find it. Validate it,” he said. “Most of the security industry was built the other way around, on the assumption that you already know where your stuff is. That gap is the whole reason CyCognito exists.”

Mapping the attacker’s view

CyCognito approaches security by working from the outside in, beginning with nothing more than a company’s name. The platform maps everything exposed to the internet, including forgotten or unmanaged assets, then tests those systems to identify weaknesses that could be exploited by attackers.

“In a nutshell, we map everything a company has exposed to the internet, then trace the handful of paths that actually lead to its internal networks and sensitive data,” Gurzeev explained.

Rather than relying solely on vulnerability scans, the platform validates which weaknesses are genuinely exploitable. According to Gurzeev, “If I had to name the one thing that makes us unique, it’s that our platform thinks like an attacker. That should be obvious. It isn’t.”

The scale of today’s attack surface

Modern enterprise environments have grown far beyond what traditional security programs were designed to manage. Gurzeev estimates that a large enterprise typically exposes around 100,000 applications, devices, and cloud assets to the internet, while some organizations have significantly larger footprints.

“Our single biggest customer has around 100 million things an attacker can interact with from the outside,” he said, adding that external attack surfaces change by one to three percent every day.

Despite that scale, many organizations continue to focus security efforts on only a small fraction of their environments. “Most security effort goes to a few hundred or a thousand key assets. What about the rest?” Gurzeev asked. “Guarding the front door while you leave the windows open is not a strategy.”

AI is expanding the problem

The rapid adoption of AI has made creating and deploying applications much easier across organizations. Employees outside traditional development teams can now build internet-facing tools using AI-powered coding assistants, often without going through established security processes.

“Today, anyone and everyone can deploy an app,” Gurzeev said. “Someone in HR or finance can spin up an application with a tool like Claude Code or Lovable and expose it to the internet, on purpose or by accident.”

He believes AI has shifted from being a supporting technology to becoming part of organizations’ core infrastructure. “As recently as six months ago, AI was bolted onto the edge of the business. Now it runs through the core, which means these systems aren’t adjacent to the attack surface anymore. They are the attack surface.”

The challenge extends beyond application growth. Gurzeev pointed to research suggesting AI-generated code introduces vulnerabilities at significantly higher rates than code written entirely by humans. More importantly, he argued that much of this software bypasses the secure development processes organizations have spent years building.

“The honest answer is we’re defending more of something less safe, and we can’t yet measure exactly how much. That uncertainty is itself the risk,” he said.

Continuous testing for an AI-driven era

As attackers increasingly adopt AI, Gurzeev argues that periodic assessments are no longer enough. Security teams need continuous visibility into what is exposed, what can actually be exploited, and which issues require immediate remediation.

“Keeping up takes three things, all continuous,” he said. “Know what you have exposed right now. Know which of it an attacker could actually break into, across all of it, not a sample. Fix what matters in hours.”

CyCognito’s latest release focuses on continuous AI security testing by combining full attack surface discovery with AI-powered validation. Rather than limiting advanced testing to a small number of high-priority assets, the platform maps an organization’s entire internet-facing environment before applying AI where reasoning is needed most.

According to Gurzeev, the platform continuously performs more than 100,000 automated checks for known issues, allowing AI to focus on identifying complex attack paths that conventional scanners often miss. As those attack chains are validated, they become repeatable automated tests, expanding coverage over time.

Looking ahead, Gurzeev remains optimistic that defenders can regain the advantage. “The winners won’t be the ones who spend the most,” he said. “They’ll be the ones who use it most efficiently, getting the most out of every dollar of compute by pointing it with context instead of running it blind. Do that, and defenders catch up.“

AI makes it trivially easy for anyone in an organization to deploy internet-facing applications, often outside established security processes. CyCognito CEO Rob Gurzeev says the resulting blind spots are more dangerous than known vulnerabilities. His answer: continuous, outside-in attack-surface mapping that validates what is actually exploitable rather than scanning a known asset list.

sentences.en: AI makes it trivially easy for anyone in an organization to deploy internet-facing applications, often outside established security processes.
sentences.zh: 人工智能使组织中的任何人都可以轻松地部署面向互联网的应用程序，通常是在既定的安全流程之外。

sentences.en: CyCognito CEO Rob Gurzeev says the resulting blind spots are more dangerous than known vulnerabilities.
sentences.zh: CyCognito 首席执行官 Rob Gurzeev 表示，由此产生的盲点比已知的漏洞更危险。

sentences.en: His answer: continuous, outside-in attack-surface mapping that validates what is actually exploitable rather than scanning a known asset list.
sentences.zh: 他的答案是：连续的、由外而内的攻击面映射，验证实际可利用的内容，而不是扫描已知的资产列表。

sentences.zh: “知道你现在暴露了什么。

sentences.en: Know which of it an attacker could actually break into, across all of it, not a sample.
sentences.zh: 了解攻击者实际上可以侵入其中的哪一个，而不是样本。

sentences.en: Fix what matters in hours.”
sentences.zh: 在数小时内解决重要问题。”

blocks.html_en: CyCognito’s latest release focuses on continuous AI security testing by combining full attack surface discovery with AI-powered validation. Rather than limiting advanced testing to a small number of high-priority assets, the platform maps an organization’s entire internet-facing environment before applying AI where reasoning is needed most.

sentences.en: CyCognito’s latest release focuses on continuous AI security testing by combining full attack surface discovery with AI-powered validation.
sentences.zh: CyCognito 的最新版本侧重于持续的 AI 安全测试，将完整的攻击面发现与 AI 驱动的验证相结合。

sentences.en: Rather than limiting advanced testing to a small number of high-priority assets, the platform maps an organization’s entire internet-facing environment before applying AI where reasoning is needed most.
sentences.zh: 该平台并没有将高级测试限制在少数高优先级资产上，而是在最需要推理的地方应用人工智能之前，映射组织的整个面向互联网的环境。

blocks.html_en: According to Gurzeev, the platform continuously performs more than 100,000 automated checks for known issues, allowing AI to focus on identifying complex attack paths that conventional scanners often miss. As those attack chains are validated, they become repeatable automated tests, expanding coverage over time.

sentences.en: According to Gurzeev, the platform continuously performs more than 100,000 automated checks for known issues, allowing AI to focus on identifying complex attack paths that conventional scanners often miss.
sentences.zh: Gurzeev 表示，该平台持续对已知问题执行超过 100,000 次自动检查，使 AI 能够专注于识别传统扫描仪经常遗漏的复杂攻击路径。

sentences.en: As those attack chains are validated, they become repeatable automated tests, expanding coverage over time.
sentences.zh: 随着这些攻击链得到验证，它们将成为可重复的自动化测试，并随着时间的推移扩大覆盖范围。

blocks.html_en: Looking ahead, Gurzeev remains optimistic that defenders can regain the advantage. “The winners won’t be the ones who spend the most,” he said. “They’ll be the ones who use it most efficiently, getting the most out of every dollar of compute by pointing it with context instead of running it blind. Do that, and defenders catch up.“

sentences.en: Looking ahead, Gurzeev remains optimistic that defenders can regain the advantage.
sentences.zh: 展望未来，古尔泽夫仍然乐观地认为防守者可以重新获得优势。

sentences.en: “The winners won’t be the ones who spend the most,” he said.
sentences.zh: “赢家不会是那些花钱最多的人，”他说。

sentences.en: “They’ll be the ones who use it most efficiently, getting the most out of every dollar of compute by pointing it with context instead of running it blind.
sentences.zh: “他们将是最有效地使用它的人，通过上下文而不是盲目运行来充分利用每一美元的计算。

sentences.en: Do that, and defenders catch up.“
sentences.zh: 这样做，防守者就会迎头赶上。”

content_html_zh:
TL;DR

人工智能使组织中的任何人都可以轻松地部署面向互联网的应用程序，通常是在既定的安全流程之外。 CyCognito 首席执行官 Rob Gurzeev 表示，由此产生的盲点比已知的漏洞更危险。 他的答案是：连续的、由外而内的攻击面映射，验证实际可利用的内容，而不是扫描已知的资产列表。

人工智能正在改变软件的构建、部署和保护方式。 虽然人工智能加速了跨行业的创新，但它也增加了组织需要保护的面向互联网的资产数量。 CyCognito 首席执行官兼联合创始人 Rob Gurzeev 表示，挑战不再只是识别已知漏洞。 它了解实际暴露的内容、这些资产如何连接以及攻击者如何利用它们。

古尔泽夫凭借多年在网络安全和情报方面的经验，相信许多组织对其攻击面的了解仍然不完整。 他说，随着人工智能使创建和公开新应用程序变得比以往任何时候都更加容易，这种差距变得越来越危险。

建立在发现未知事物之上的安全心态

早在人工智能出现之前，古尔泽夫就开始涉足网络安全领域。 十几岁的时候，他花时间探索计算机和互联网中继聊天 (IRC) 社区，对黑客的好奇心首先在这些社区中产生。 这种兴趣最终使他进入了情报部门，在那里从事侦察和攻击面行动。

“老实说，这项工作选择了我，而不是我选择了它，”古尔泽夫说。 他解释说，这个角色通常只是从一个名字开始，需要找到“阻力最小的途径来完成重要的事情”。

这些经历继续影响着他今天处理网络安全的方式。 “我被教导说你永远不知道现实是什么。 你必须去找它。 验证一下，”他说。 “大多数安全行业都是以相反的方式建立的，假设你已经知道你的东西在哪里。 这种差距就是 CyCognito 存在的全部原因。”

绘制攻击者的视角

CyCognito 通过从外到内的方式实现安全性，从公司名称开始。 该平台映射暴露在互联网上的所有内容，包括被遗忘或不受管理的资产，然后测试这些系统以识别可能被攻击者利用的弱点。

“简而言之，我们绘制公司暴露在互联网上的所有内容，然后追踪实际通往其内部网络和敏感数据的少数路径，”古尔泽夫解释道。

该平台不是仅仅依赖于漏洞扫描，而是验证哪些漏洞是真正可利用的。 Gurzeev 表示：“如果让我说出我们的独特之处，那就是我们的平台像攻击者一样思考。 这应该是显而易见的。 事实并非如此。”

当今攻击面的规模

现代企业环境的发展远远超出了传统安全程序的设计管理范围。 Gurzeev 估计，大型企业通常会向互联网公开约 100,000 个应用程序、设备和云资产，而一些组织的足迹要大得多。

“我们最大的单一客户拥有大约 1 亿个东西，攻击者可以从外部与之交互，”他说，并补充说外部攻击面每天都会发生 1% 到 3% 的变化。

尽管规模如此之大，许多组织仍然将安全工作仅集中在其环境的一小部分上。 “大多数安全工作都集中在数百或数千个关键资产上。 剩下的人呢？” 古尔泽夫问道。 “当你把窗户打开时，守住前门并不是一个策略。”

人工智能正在扩大问题范围

人工智能的快速采用使得跨组织创建和部署应用程序变得更加容易。 传统开发团队之外的员工现在可以使用人工智能驱动的编码助手构建面向互联网的工具，通常无需经过既定的安全流程。

“今天，任何人都可以部署应用程序，”古尔泽夫说。 “人力资源或财务人员可以使用 Claude Code 或 Lovable 等工具启动应用程序，并将其有意或无意地暴露在互联网上。”

他认为人工智能已经从一种支持技术转变为组织核心基础设施的一部分。 “就在六个月前，人工智能还被固定在业务的边缘。 现在它贯穿核心，这意味着这些系统不再与攻击面相邻。 他们是攻击面。”

挑战不仅仅限于应用程序增长。 古尔泽夫指出，研究表明人工智能生成的代码引入漏洞的速度明显高于完全由人类编写的代码。 更重要的是，他认为大部分软件都绕过了组织花费数年时间构建的安全开发流程。

“诚实的答案是，我们正在保护更多不太安全的东西，但我们还无法准确衡量保护程度。 这种不确定性本身就是风险，”他说。

人工智能驱动时代的持续测试

古尔泽夫认为，随着攻击者越来越多地采用人工智能，定期评估已经不够了。 安全团队需要持续了解暴露的内容、实际可利用的内容以及需要立即修复的问题。

“跟上需要三件事，而且都是持续的，”他说。 “知道你现在暴露了什么。 了解攻击者实际上可以侵入其中的哪一个，而不是样本。 在数小时内解决重要问题。”

CyCognito 的最新版本侧重于持续的 AI 安全测试，将完整的攻击面发现与 AI 驱动的验证相结合。 该平台并没有将高级测试限制在少数高优先级资产上，而是在最需要推理的地方应用人工智能之前，映射组织的整个面向互联网的环境。

Gurzeev 表示，该平台持续对已知问题执行超过 100,000 次自动检查，使 AI 能够专注于识别传统扫描仪经常遗漏的复杂攻击路径。 随着这些攻击链得到验证，它们将成为可重复的自动化测试，并随着时间的推移扩大覆盖范围。

展望未来，古尔泽夫仍然乐观地认为防守者可以重新获得优势。 “赢家不会是那些花钱最多的人，”他说。 “他们将是最有效地使用它的人，通过上下文而不是盲目运行来充分利用每一美元的计算。 这样做，防守者就会迎头赶上。”
